messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following not-for-profit food bank data collection notice for GDPR compliance:

"FoodFirst collects the name, household composition, and dietary requirements of individuals and families accessing our services. We collect this information to provide appropriate food parcels and plan our service capacity. Data is not shared with any third parties. Dietary requirements including allergies and religious restrictions are collected with your verbal consent. Records are kept for 12 months and then deleted."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) or vital interests (Art. 6(1)(d)) implied for service delivery to vulnerable individuals. Neither is explicitly stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Dietary requirements including religious restrictions constitute data revealing religious beliefs — special category data under Art. 9(1). Verbal consent is described but is not documented or verifiable, and the Art. 9(2) basis is not explicitly stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Food parcel provision and service capacity planning are clear and specific purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Name, household composition, and dietary requirements are proportionate to the service delivery purpose.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 12-month retention with deletion is clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] No data subject rights mentioned.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] "Not shared with any third parties" is clearly stated.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Not applicable given no third-party sharing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Processing of vulnerable individuals' data at scale may require a DPO.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not stated), Art. 9 (religious dietary data requires documented Art. 9(2) basis — verbal consent is insufficient without records), Art. 15-21 (rights absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State explicit lawful basis — likely vital interests (Art. 6(1)(d)) for vulnerable individuals in need or legitimate interest
- Replace verbal consent with documented written or digital consent for religious/dietary special category data
- Add a simple data subject rights section appropriate to service users
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
